title: Stage-1 sim-to-real triad (real/synth/synth+DA -> real): the honest gap number
description: One supervised defect-seg U-Net, vary training source, shared real eval half. real->real=ceiling, synth->real=gap (train on our synthetic defects, test real), synth+DA->real=closure. rgb channel. The headline Stage-1 deliverable.
close_reason: Sim-to-real triad complete: gap 27pp, AdaBN closes 46% + restores calibration, curriculum negative documented. 3-seed, in RESULTS + learning note.

title: Stage 2 — Isaac Sim RTX 5090 viability spike (gate for the digital-twin render)
description:
1-day spike to confirm Isaac Sim runs for synthetic-data gen on the RTX 5090 (Blackwell sm_120) before committing Stage 1 to it. If it fights, fall back to BlenderProc MVP.

PINNED WORKING CONFIG (from IsaacLab #1888):
- Isaac Sim 5.1.0 + Isaac Lab, NATIVE WINDOWS (not WSL — WSL wedges on this box; Isaac 5.1 supports native Windows, WSL2 only needed for ROS2 Humble).
- torch 2.7.0 nightly cu128 (pip install --pre torch --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/nightly/cu128); ignore incompatible-version warnings.
- NVIDIA driver ~570.86+ / 580 / 590 (avoid 5.1.0-rc.19 + 580-open = startup crash). Use Interactive Path Tracing (standard render = noisy viewport on Blackwell).

KNOWN BUG TO DODGE (IsaacLab #4951, open Mar 2026): TiledCamera HANGS on RTX 5090 in the omni.replicator tiled-render path. Workaround: use standard Camera (not TiledCamera) — identical RGB, fine for single-scene data-gen. TiledCamera only matters for massively-parallel RL (the B/Isaac-Lab path), not our Replicator label-gen.

SPIKE PASS CRITERIA:
1. Isaac Sim launches native-Windows on the 5090 with pinned config.
2. Replicator renders ONE scene with standard Camera -> emits RGB + depth + semantic-seg passes.
3. PASS -> commit Stage 1 (synth defect engine) to Isaac (USD/Omniverse lane signal + B substrate). FAIL -> BlenderProc MVP now, revisit Isaac when Blackwell bugs settle.
notes: PARKED 2026-07-05. CRITERION #1 (kernel boots on 595.79) PASSED via isaacsim 5.1->6.0.1.0 upgrade (no driver downgrade). CRITERION #2 (one labeled frame) BLOCKED at render. Correct 6.0 API established: rep.functional.create.* (immediate prims), rep.annotators.get(), set_capture_on_play(False). Config matrix all fail: [async ON+step]->empty; [async OFF+timeline.play+step]->'renderer failed to advance to scheduled frame'; [async OFF+step only]->empty. Kernel/RTX/Replicator init fine; the frame render won't fill annotators. NEXT-SESSION HYPOTHESES (focused, not smoke): (1) rep.orchestrator.step(rt_subframes=N, delta_time=..); (2) BasicWriter + rep.orchestrator.run() + app.update() drain-loop (the documented writer path, not annotators); (3) render-time may need the 6.0-TESTED Windows driver 581.42 even though BOOT works on 595.79 (Blackwell render vs boot); (4) pip-package vs full workstation-install difference. Script: sim/smoke_replicator.py.

title: Ruff Phase 5 — advisory tools: vulture (dead fns) + coverage (untested)
description: After ruff. vulture: dead functions, advisory, scoped to pure modules (core/, surfscan/evaluation). coverage: untested surface, advisory. Call out shared blind spot: dead code only a TEST references looks live to both. Advisory-first (|| true), don't gate initially.
acceptance_criteria: vulture + coverage wired advisory in CI; a pass run + findings triaged; merged
notes:
VULTURE + COVERAGE — PROVEN INTEGRATION (ported from cardiac-seg PR #5, 2026-07-08). Lint/scan paths here: core surfscan.

VULTURE (two-tier, in the lint job):
- RED (blocks): `uvx vulture@2.16 --min-confidence 80` — near-certain only (redundant conditions, unreachable, unused locals). min_confidence = how sure vulture is its dead-guess is RIGHT (unused-func=60% since it cannot prove no dynamic/cross-file caller; redundant-if=100%), NOT how-dead. 80 = ~zero FPs, no whitelist, but misses dead FUNCTIONS.
- YELLOW (warns): `--min-confidence 60` piped through sed to `::warning file=,line=::` annotations, in a `continue-on-error: true` step. GitHub has NO native yellow check for run steps; yellow = warning annotations, check stays green. CRITICAL: sed line in a BLOCK SCALAR (`run: |`) — a bare `run:` with the sed's ': ' colon-space breaks YAML parse and the WHOLE workflow silently fails to parse (jobs never run). Validate ci.yml with yaml.safe_load before pushing.
- [tool.vulture]: paths = core surfscan (NOT tests — a prod symbol referenced only by a test surfaces as dead here instead of being masked by test fan-in; the trap that fools BOTH tools). ignore_names=["model_config"] + ignore_decorators=["@model_validator","@field_validator","@field_serializer","@model_serializer","@computed_field"] kill pydantic FPs. exclude same dirs as ruff. Hunt PROD-dead code by scanning WITHOUT tests; with-vs-without diff = the test-only set.

COVERAGE (pytest-cov, two-tier):
- FOLD into ONE run: `pytest tests --cov --cov-report=term-missing` = test gate AND coverage data. Do NOT add a separate coverage job (re-runs the suite = heavier). Then `coverage report --fail-under=80` (RED, blocks) + `coverage report --fail-under=95 || echo ::warning::...` in `continue-on-error: true` + `if: always()` (YELLOW). Tiers: red<80 / yellow 80-95 / green>=95.
- [tool.coverage.run] source=core+surfscan; omit only genuine entry/IO scripts. [tool.coverage.report] exclude_lines drops `if __name__=='__main__':` + protocol stubs. pytest-cov -> dev+all extras; uv lock.

THE HONESTY PRINCIPLE (owner, load-bearing): DO NOT raise coverage by omitting whole modules. Logic/eval MUST be tested (wrong logic = wrong numbers). Think in FUNCTIONS with inputs+outputs, not files: a good separation is easy to test (pure logic split from I/O); a bad one binds logic to file/GPU/mlflow. Where a fn MIXES I/O with computation, EXTRACT the pure input->output core and TEST it with equivalence classes; leave a thin I/O shell. `# pragma: no cover` ONLY for genuine external-dependency lines (GPU/cupy/cucim, mlflow network, plt/pyvista render, real-dataset disk reads) — NEVER to dodge testing pure logic.

TESTS: equivalence-class discipline — partition input space, one rep per class + boundaries; each test docstring names the class; terse asserts. Partial coverage usually = happy-path-only existing tests; ADD the missing branches/classes, don't rewrite.

CI HYGIENE (proven): single trigger `on: push:[main] + pull_request:[main]` (a dev push fires only the PR event -> ONE run not two; pull_request tests the MERGE PREVIEW). `concurrency: {group: ci-${{ github.ref }}, cancel-in-progress: true}`. Bonus: the FIRST pytest-cov run surfaced a real broken test import (stale symbol) failing collection — coverage doubles as an import-health check.

title: Ruff Phase 4 — robustness + complexity (FIX, don't defer)
description: Owner fixes, does not gate-skip. BLE001+S110: specific exception or let crash (know what throws). C901+PLR0912/0915: split / strategy-pattern. PLR0913(12): collapse to config objects (dataclass/pydantic), not param lists. FBT: bools keyword-only. RUF100 last: rip dead noqa. Graduate each.
acceptance_criteria: all listed families clean in core surfscan + in enforced --select; CI green; merged (multiple PRs)
close_reason: Ruff ratchet complete: full curated select enforced at zero, all phases 0-4 merged

title: Ruff Phase 3 — principled fixes: PLR2004 magics + PLC0415 imports + E702 (~71)
description: 3a PLR2004(33): name magic-value literals as module constants (_THR-style). 3b PLC0415(15): hoist lazy imports to top; circulars -> break by EXTRACTION never lazy. 3c E702(23): split a;b semicolon one-liners. Graduate PLR2004, PLC0415, E7.
acceptance_criteria: PLR2004+PLC0415+E7 clean in core surfscan; appended to enforced --select; CI green; merged (may be 2-3 PRs)
close_reason: PLC0415 hoisted + PLR2004 named, graduated (#6)

title: Ruff Phase 2 — T201 prints -> core/obs logging module (47)
description: Build core/obs.py (cardiac core.obs analog): thin logging wrapper, plain formatter so CLI runners stay human-readable (harness tables, triad_summary) — convert, NOT per-file-ignore. Replace all 47 prints in experiments/, visualization/, evaluation/{harness,results,sync_numbers,modelcard}, training/train, core/data/store. Graduate T201.
acceptance_criteria: no print() in core surfscan; core/obs.py added; T201 in enforced --select; CI green; merged
close_reason: T201 -> core/obs logging, 38 prints converted, graduated F,I,B,T201 (#5)

title: Ruff Phase 1 — B (bugbear) + auto-fix + trivial (~30)
description: Auto-fix PLR0402(7)/RUF022/046/C420/UP035 (~11). Manual-trivial: B905 strict=(6), B023 loop-var(1), E741(2), RUF059 unused-unpack(4). Fix all B in core surfscan, graduate B into enforced --select. Bare noqa only if unavoidable.
acceptance_criteria: B clean in core surfscan; B appended to enforced --select + pyproject; CI green; merged
close_reason: B fixed (B905 strict=, B023 bind) + graduated to enforced gate F,I,B (#4)

title: Ruff Phase 0 — [tool.ruff] config + per-file-ignores (kills S101/N806, ~62)
description: Add [tool.ruff] to pyproject: line-length=120, extend-exclude=['sim'], [tool.ruff.lint].select=curated full set, per-file-ignores tests/**=[S101,PLR2004,FBT] + __init__.py=[F401]. Do NOT select N (N806/803/812 = B/H/W/C tensor-dim idiom; owner sign-off first, else per-file-ignore tensor modules). Enforced --select stays F,I; advisory now shows true curated backlog. Config-only, ~62 vanish (S101 all-in-tests + N806).
acceptance_criteria: pyproject [tool.ruff] present; S101+N806 gone from advisory; CI green; PR merged dev->main
close_reason: [tool.ruff] curated config + per-file-ignores merged (#3)

title: [epic] Grow the code-quality gate — extend the ruff ratchet + conventions (from cardiac-seg)
description:
Grow the code-quality gate to match cardiac-seg (PRs #1-4). synthscape ALREADY has the CI structure + an early ratchet: ci.yml lint job runs ruff 0.15.13 check core surfscan --select F,I. This epic EXTENDS that ratchet to the full family set + adopts the conventions. Lint paths: core surfscan.

INTEGRATION PATH (extend the existing ratchet; cardiac-seg-proven order):
Already enforced: F, I. Add each family, one cluster per PR, driving it to 0 by FIXING first (owner: fix-all philosophy), then appending its code to the enforced --select:
  B (bugbear; B009 = getattr-with-constant) -> T201 (no prints, use logging) -> PLR2004 (name magic numbers) -> PLC0415 (imports at top; fix circular imports by EXTRACTION, never lazy imports) -> BLE001 + S110 (no blind except: specific exception or let it crash; know what CAN throw) -> C901 + PLR0912/0915 (complexity: split / strategy) -> PLR0913 (too-many-args: config objects) -> FBT (bools keyword-only) -> RUF100 (no dead noqa).
Keep the two-step CI shape: ENFORCED --select (blocks) + ADVISORY full ruff check . --statistics (|| true, surfaces the remaining families). pyproject [tool.ruff.lint].select = the curated full set; E501 advisory if a dense style makes 120 painful.

CONVENTIONS (owner guidance from the cardiac-seg review threads):
- noqa: bare form only, NO prose reasons. RUF100 enforces that a noqa suppresses a REAL violation.
- Owner is AGAINST COMMENTS: minimal, self-documenting code + names (a comment jammed mid-expression once silently dead-coded a term = real bug).
- getattr/hasattr = smell: direct attribute access; B009 catches the no-default form; with-default + hasattr need review.
- CONFIG POLYMORPHISM (if synthscape has strategy-selecting configs): pydantic DISCRIMINATED UNIONS, each variant owns its params + a virtual build() (cfg.build()), not kind/type dispatch. kind = serialization discriminator only. --set variant shim + _lift backward-compat validator.
- Linter = BUG-FINDER: fix what it surfaces (in cardiac-seg it caught an F821 NameError + a comment-jam that dead-coded a physics term).

ADVISORY tools after ruff (own sub-beads): vulture (dead functions) + coverage (untested), advisory-first, scoped to pure modules. Shared blind spot: a dead product fn only a TEST references looks live to both.

Phased PRs, dev->main, each green. Mirror CLAUDE.md convention edits into AGENTS.md.
close_reason: Ruff ratchet complete: full curated select enforced at zero, all phases 0-4 merged

title: Closed-loop curriculum: adapt synth defect difficulty to detector weakness
description: Port the acoustic-engine differentiator. Per-epoch, read per-defect-kind val score; reweight synth sampling toward the detector's current weak kinds (or adapt subtlety). Novel in 3D sim-to-real.
notes: Ported + demonstrated, but REGRESSED (au_pro 0.487 vs uniform 0.537, ECE doubled). Honest negative kept in repo. Fix (Stage-1.5): adapt difficulty not kind.
close_reason: Ported + measured; negative result (kind-chasing hurt) documented in RESULTS + learning note

title: DA method: AdaBN (recompute BN stats on real, unlabeled) for the closure arm
description: Reproduce one named domain-adaptation method. AdaBN (Li 2017): swap BatchNorm running stats to the target (real) domain at test — cheap, unsupervised, standard. Feeds synth+DA->real arm of the triad.
close_reason: Closed

title: Stage 2 — DIGITAL TWIN: reconstruct good objects -> renderable 3D asset + Isaac render
description: The ambitious road AND the 3D-reconstruction job-lane portfolio piece (dual-purpose). Reconstruct a clean renderable 3D asset per category from the 244 single-view good instances. NOTE: NOT multi-view fusion -- MVTec is 244 DIFFERENT objects, one view each (verified: bagel/train/good = 244 single-view instances). So it's a LEARNED category-shape model / single-view completion (unseen back via symmetry/shape priors), using the 244 to learn the class shape -- not SfM/MVS. Then (needs Isaac -> depends on p3h) render arbitrary good + defect views -> more train-good data + the full sim-to-real triad. DUAL-PURPOSE: feeds the synth engine AND is direct evidence for the reconstruction roles in PLAN.local (Fiducial=Gaussian-splat/bundle-adj, Productize=SfM/MVS, 3DLOOK=body-recon). Harder -> do AFTER Road 1a. HONEST: won't beat the good-only PatchCore 0.908 -- it's for the contribution (sim-to-real) + portfolio, not the metric. Prior art: single-view shape completion, category shape priors; 3DGS/NeRF need multi-view (N/A here).

title: Stage 1 — CLASSICAL synthetic defects on real good scans (Isaac-free)
description: The pragmatic first road — no reconstruction, no Isaac, real-pixel base transfers better than an imperfect sim. Inject defects directly on real good scans in 2.5D: carve holes / displace bumps in xyz, add contamination/scratches in rgb, cracks -> labeled synth defect data in MVTec format (via core/data/synth.py). Train-on-synth-defects (DRAEM + supervised feature methods) -> the synth->real gap number. Directly fixes the DRAEM-on-crude-Perlin failure (RESULTS.md 0.48: discriminator learned 'spot noise' not defects) with REALISTIC injected defects. Prior art: DRAEM / CutPaste / NSA (synthetic anomalies on real normals). Feeds the triad (17p). No Isaac dependency -- can start now, driver-free.
notes: CORRECTION (2026-07-06): earlier note over-claimed 'realism hurts DRAEM'. Established fact is narrower: MY v2 synth (0.18±0.02) < Perlin (0.36±0.09) for DRAEM-on-xyz, 3-seed. WHY is unresolved — my synth is geometrically suspect (z-axis displacement not surface-normal; magnitude 15-35% of z-range = giant gouge; xyz-only so rgb-appearance untested). Likely a bad synth, not a realism law. Also holds: RESULTS.md 0.48 was a single lucky seed (true ~0.36). Disentangle: (1) visualize a synth bagel (dent or shear-crater?), (2) normal-displacement + sensible magnitude, (3) vary contrast. See learning/2026-07-06_draem-synthesis-comparison.md. Engine (core/data/defects.py) + harness (run_draem --synth/--seed) built+tested.
close_reason: Done: core/data/defects.py (normal-displaced injection) + comparison merged; realistic vs Perlin resolved (RESULTS #1)

title: Eval: modelcard.py — auto-generate a per-run model card from artifacts
description: systole's cardioseg/evaluation/modelcard.py auto-generates a MODEL_CARD from run artifacts so the card always matches the shipped model. mirage has the export extra but no modelcard. On-mission (PLAN mentions model cards + parity-gated ONNX export at train-end).
close_reason: Done — surfscan/evaluation/modelcard.py auto-generates docs/MODEL_CARD.md from a run.

title: Arch: results.py — refresh docs/RESULTS.json measured entries from MLflow
description: Complete the single-source-numbers pattern (systole's cardioseg/evaluation/results.py): a results.py that queries MLflow for the latest run per method and refreshes the measured au_pro/img_auroc entries in docs/RESULTS.json (keeping paradigm labels + SOTA cite as curated constants), then sync_numbers renders the docs. Today RESULTS.json is hand-curated from the measured investigation; this makes reruns auto-flow to the docs.
close_reason: Done — surfscan/evaluation/results.py refreshes RESULTS.json measured entries from mlflow (run when canonical full-10 runs are in mlflow, not the current smoke runs).

title: M3DM multimodal: learned point-transformer + rgb fusion (the real path to SOTA 0.96)
description: The measured route to close 0.91->0.96: M3DM-style — PointMAE/point-transformer geometry features (learned, not hand-FPFH) + rgb, multi-memory-bank fusion. Big build (pretrained point backbone + fusion). Only worth it if a SOTA number is the goal; the honest investigation + 0.91 detector is already the defensible Stage-0 deliverable.

title: Eval: calibration (ECE under shift) + per-condition (defect-type) diagnostics
description: The eval-rigor contribution beyond AUROC/AU-PRO: ECE/reliability of the anomaly score under domain shift, and per-defect-type / per-material stratified failure (like systole stratified EF by pathology). Carried calibration toolbox from acoustic.
notes: Per-defect-type diagnostics DONE (evaluation/diagnostics.py, wired into run_patchcore). ECE/calibration still pending (needs a score->probability calibration step).
close_reason: Closed

title: BTF: native-resolution FPFH (close the geometry gap)
description: Our BTF is preprocessing-limited (AU-PRO 0.653 vs paper ~0.96): FPFH runs on the 256-resized/normalized/grazing-noisy cloud. Try native-res clean point clouds + tuned FPFH radius + drop grazing-noise points (synthscape-78p). Better geometry -> better fusion.
notes: ATTEMPTED + backfired: BTF @512 + grazing-clean = 0.355 vs @256 0.513. FPFH density-sensitive (fixed knn shrinks at higher res), grazing-clean dropped legit surface. Bigger truth: hand-rolled FPFH won't reach SOTA — M3DM uses a LEARNED point-transformer. Closing 0.91->0.96 via FPFH is a low-ceiling rabbit hole. REAL path = M3DM (point-transformer + multimodal fusion) = a separate big build. Deprioritize FPFH tuning; deployable stays PatchCore-rgb ~0.91.

title: PatchCore + fusion: greedy coreset, fused all-10, deployable detector (toward SOTA)
description: PatchCore-rgb is the working method (AU-PRO 0.908). Push to ~0.96: greedy k-center coreset (vs random), run fused rgb+geometry over all 10 (bagel proved +1pt -> 0.937), pick the deployable detector. The 'compare to normal' winner.
notes:
DONE: fused all-10 (run_fused.py) = AU-PRO 0.904, net-neutral vs rgb-only 0.908 (helps where geometry decent, hurts where weak). Deployable = rgb-only feature methods (PatchCore / feature-recon, both 0.908). REMAINING: greedy k-center coreset (vs random); fusion only pays once BTF geometry improves (synthscape-lbf).
DIRECTION (the drill): harden PatchCore-rgb (0.908) to its real ceiling toward SOTA 0.96 — no new methods. Levers: (1) locally-aware features (neighborhood avg-pool, standard PatchCore), (2) greedy k-center coreset vs random, (3) resolution (256-resize caps localization; try higher-res store), (4) decent geometry fusion once BTF native-res lands. Refactor the duplicated run_* loops into one eval harness while at it.

title: Baseline: BTF (quarantined SOTA, FPFH memory bank)
description: The systole nnU-Net move: BTF (Back to the Feature) = handcrafted FPFH + RGB in a PatchCore-style memory bank (~0.96 AU-PRO, no training). Run it quarantined via the 3D-ADS repo (github.com/eliahuhorwitz/3D-ADS), score through OUR eval harness. The honest SOTA-floor our deployable model is reported against. baselines/btf/.
close_reason: Closed

title: Recon #6: diffusion reconstruction (generative flex)
description: ORDER 5 (ambitious/optional). Diffusion trained on normal; test = noise->denoise heals anomalies toward normal, diff = anomaly. Plays the generative strength (AudioLDM/Stable-Audio transfer). Differentiator, later.

title: Recon #5: DRAEM-synthesis (reconstruct + discriminate, ties to Stage-1 engine)
description: ORDER 4. Synthesize fake defects on normal samples -> train restore-normal + segment-defect. SOTA-competitive (DAS3D/3DSR are 3D DRAEM). THE convergence point with the Stage-1 synthetic engine (synthscape-17p).
notes: CORRECTION: DRAEM is a self-contained STAGE-0 method, NOT Stage-1-gated. Its synthesis = lightweight proxy anomalies (Perlin masks + texture/cut-paste corruption) inside the method, to train the discriminator — a few lines of augmentation, independent of the Stage-1 physics engine. Buildable now alongside inpaint/feature-recon. Stage-1's realistic defects are an OPTIONAL later upgrade to its synthesis (the DAS3D/3DSR path), a one-way enhancement, not a dependency.
close_reason: Closed

title: Recon #4: feature-reconstruction / reverse-distillation (RD4AD-style)
description: ORDER 3. Frozen encoder + trained decoder rebuilds the encoder FEATURES from a bottleneck; anomaly = feature-space error. Strong 2D-AD family, avoids blurry-pixel problem. Shares harness + eval.
close_reason: Closed

title: Recon #3: masked-inpainting model (RIAD/MAE-style)
description: ORDER 2. Mask random grid patches, train to inpaint from context; test = badly-inpainted regions are anomalous. Kills the identity shortcut, grid-native. The cheap real bump after the VAE. Shares harness + eval.
notes: Built + evaluated (bagel): AU-PRO 0.014, unchanged from VAE. Inpainting did NOT fix localization — raw-xyz residual tracks geometric complexity (rim/grazing) not defect-ness; smooth defects get low residual regardless of masking. Reconstruction-in-raw-space is weak here (measured across 3 variants). Real levers: rgb/fused modality, or feature-space/memory-bank residual.

title: Recon #1: vanilla conv-AE (the strawman floor)
description: ORDER 1b (optional). Plain conv AE on the xyz grid, anomaly = per-pixel recon error. The identity-shortcut strawman; honest floor. Shares the harness from synthscape-y83. May fold into the VAE task as an ablation.
close_reason: Closed

title: Grazing-angle normal noise: tighten preprocess if the VAE cares
description: From the --normals viz (2026-06-26): where the surface tilts away from the scanner, structured-light sampling is sparse/noisy with depth-quantization banding -> garbage normals (PCA can't fit a stable plane). Centroid-clip+SOR+erosion don't catch it. Option: drop low-normal-confidence / non-scanner-facing points. DEFER until the VAE shows whether it matters (noise is consistent across train+test). See learning/2026-06-25_fpfh-and-neighborhoods.md.

title: Axis 1: segmentation (keep reachable for the platform role)
description: Dense spatial labeling on the breadth axis; shares the eval harness. The lever that completes the CV-platform target. Optional, after the spine.

title: Stage 3 — real-world integration (edge deploy: ONNX -> TensorRT + benchmark)
description: ONNX export with parity check (train-vs-deploy skew discipline, carried), TensorRT, FPS/latency/memory. Target <1% AUROC/AU-PRO loss vs full precision.

title: Stage 1: synthetic defect engine + sim-to-real triad
description: Stage 1: the sim-to-real TRIAD = the measured result both roads feed (the contribution, not a leaderboard number). Same detectors, three data settings: (1) real->real = the ceiling (HAVE it: PatchCore 0.908); (2) synth->real = the gap (headline honest number); (3) synth+DA->real = gap closure (reproduce ONE domain-adaptation method + the carried closed-loop curriculum). Two roads produce the synth: 9gy (classical defect-injection on real, Isaac-free, start here) + b3y (digital-twin reconstruction + Isaac render). This bead = the triad table that scores whatever they produce, through the existing harness.
close_reason: Stage-1 triad delivered via the classical road (9gy); merged PR #2. Isaac p3h is Stage-2, not a blocker.

title: pointcloud-viewer (web, cardioview analog)
description: three.js/Open3D-web: predicted anomaly heatmap on the 3D cloud, drop-your-own-scan, in-browser ONNX. Built only once there is a model output to show.
close_reason: Closed

title: Stage 0: anomaly scoring (residual + latent Mahalanobis)
description: Per-pixel reconstruction residual -> pixel anomaly map (AU-PRO); top-residual aggregate -> image score (AUROC); latent Mahalanobis as a second score.
notes: Residual map + top-k image score done (evaluation/scoring.py). Pending: latent Mahalanobis as the second score.

title: Stage 0: eval harness (the contribution)
description: image AUROC, pixel AU-PRO, calibration (ECE) under shift, per-condition diagnostics (defect type/category). The differentiator; carried from acoustic eval. nnU-Net-style: PatchCore/M3DM quarantined baseline through the SAME eval.
notes: Image AUROC + pixel AU-PRO (per-region envelope, metric self-verified perfect=1/random=0.17) + per-category + results.json + MLflow done. Pending: calibration (ECE), more per-condition diagnostics (defect-type).
close_reason: Closed

title: Stage 0: reconstruction VAE on the position-map grid
description: Build the VAE (ported from acoustic anomaly work): encoder/decoder on the organized xyz grid; masked reconstruction loss (valid pixels only); train on good only. Geometry-only (3ch) first; rgb/fused later.
notes:
MODEL #2 in the reconstruction ladder + the shared GPU-resident harness. Build: mirage/data/dataset.py (whole split -> one VRAM tensor, on-device batching), mirage/models/vae.py (conv VAE on the 256 xyz grid), mirage/training/{hparams,losses,train}.py. Efficiency: dataset fits in 32GB VRAM -> GPU-resident whole-epoch (acoustic trick), bf16 autocast, channels_last, TF32, torch.compile, big batch. Train on good only. Hardware: RTX 5090 32GB, 32 cores, 100GB RAM.
Harness + VAE built and smoke-tested (bagel: loss 1.38->0.67, 0.07s/epoch, RTX 5090). Remaining for this task: train full dataset + wire anomaly scoring/eval (separate beads 6v3/2zm).
Reconstruction family (VAE/dropout/inpaint/fused) all AU-PRO ~0.014 on bagel — raw-residual tracks geometric complexity not defect-ness. PIVOT: PatchCore feature memory bank -> AU-PRO 0.928 (66x). Feature 'compare-to-normal' is the working paradigm; reconstruction is the honest weak floor.
close_reason: Closed

title: Data loader/store: unified raw->processed (MVTec 3D-AD)
description: mirage/data: adapter (mvtec.py) + preprocess contract (background mask, flying-pixel removal, bbox crop, resize, physical-scale normalize) + store.py (processed/mvtec3d/<paramkey>/ data/*.npz + unified meta.csv). DONE this commit; verify full-dataset build.
close_reason: Closed

title: Arch: baselines/ separation — the quarantine-ceiling methods out of models/
description: Both siblings keep baselines/ (the standard reported methods they quarantine against — nnU-Net, CSP+LDA) SEPARATE from the science package. mirage mixes the SOTA-baseline memory-bank methods (PatchCore/BTF) with the recon family in surfscan/models/. Consider a baselines/ for the quarantine ceiling. Less clear-cut here (PatchCore IS the deployable), so lower priority.
close_reason: WON'T-DO — baselines/ mis-models mirage: siblings' baselines/ hold an external SOTA method they RUN (nnU-Net, CSP+LDA); mirage's methods are all ours and its external ref (M3DM) is a paper, not run. PatchCore is the deployable, not a baseline. Forcing baselines/ = cargo-cult, violates 'no speculative folders'.

title: Arch: core/ vs surfscan/ split — separate reusable engine from science layer
description: mindscape (newest sibling) splits core/ (config, data, store, splits, adapters, the Method contract, export_onnx = reusable engine) from neuroscan/ (harness, metrics, calibrate, models, tracking = the science/contribution). mirage is monolithic surfscan/. Consider extracting surfscan/{config,data} -> core/ so the reusable data/config plumbing is separable from the ML science. Bigger refactor; do when it earns its keep.
close_reason: Done — core/ split committed (ebb2997): core/ = config·data·method, surfscan/ = science.
